info.title: DirectIt API
info.description:
# Introduction
 This API reference provides a reference to integrate your system to the DirectIT system. The API allows you to create, get, and update orders, and handle accounts and other references. 

You can access to API tool for testing [here](https://api.directx.ca/api-docs)
 ## Base URL
The base URL for all the API endpoints is `https://api.directx.ca`

For example
* `https://api.directx.ca/orders/1000`
* `https://api.directx.ca/zpl/1000/12345`
* `https://api.directx.ca/referenceFields/1000` 

# Use Case 
 ### 1 Register for account 
 Contact IT Direct to register for account 
 ### 2 Obtain token 
 You can retrieve a __SSO Token__ by entering your credentials and account info in the [generateLoginToken](#operation/generateLoginToken) call. Your sso token will then be emailed to you. 
 ### 3 Create order 
 Create an order using [postOrder](#operation/postOrder) and include sso_token in authentication. 

 After the call, the reponse will return the reference to the order which will be its __jobID__. The jobID is used when making other calls to update or retrieve information for the order. 
 ### 4 Print label 
 ZPL Label image can be retrieved using the call [https://api.directx.ca/zpl/{account}/{jobID}](#operation/zpl)
 ### 5 Get reference 
 Get reference fields for your account. 
 ### 6 Track an order 
 Examples of API calls to use are: 
 * `https://api.directx.ca/order/{account}/{jobID}` 
 * `https://api.directx.ca/barcode/{jobID}`
 * `https://api.directx.ca/orderPhoto/{account}/{jobID}` 
 # Sample Code 
 ## 1 Register for an account 
  Contact IT Direct to register for account. 
## 2 Obtain token 
 Construct a patch request with the baseURL: `https://api.directx.ca/generateLoginToken`.

 In the payload, enter the credentials. 
```
{
    "email": "user@example.com",
    "user_name": "string",
    "account": "1000"
}
```
 Check email for the sso_token. 
 ## 3 Create order 
 Description on each of the data fields for postOrder can be found here: [postOrder](#operation/postOrder). 

 Send a post to url `https://api.directx.ca/postOrder`. 

 Enter in the [sso_token](#section/Authentication/Using-SSO-Token) in the header for authentication.

Enter in the payload: 
 ```
{
"account": "1000",
"shipAddressID": 1111,
"conAddressID": 1111,
"del_inst": "Leave at door",
"cust_ref": "John Doe",
"checkedEmail": true,
"deliveryEmail": "johndoe@example.com",
"rate": {
   "baseRate": 3,
   "ser_time": 10,
   "ser_area": "S",
   "dtdct": "2019-08-24T14:15:22Z",
   "dtCallIn": "2019-08-24T14:15:22Z"
},
"email": "user@example.com",
"pieces": 0,
"weight": 0,
"packageType": "Envelope",
"caller": "string",
"additionalEmail": "user@example.com",
"returnTripService": {
   "baseRate": 0,
   "ser_time": 0,
   "dtdct": "2019-08-24T14:15:22Z"
}
}
```
 
 If the order was successfully created, it will return a JSON response with the values: 
```
{
    "companyID": 1234,
    "jobID": "12345",    "foxJob": "1111",
    "email_req": "johndoe@example.com",
    "email_addr": "johndoe@example.com",
    "ship_addressID": 10000,
    "trackingNumber": "1234567890",
    "isEmailSent": 1,
    "result": "Finished",
    "returnJobID": "54321",
    "returnTrackingNumber": "1234554321",
    "returnFoxJob": "10007",
    "returnResult": "Pending"
}
```
 The jobID reference for this order is `12345`. This value will be used when accessing data for the order.
  ## 4 Print label 
 ZPL Label image can be retrieved using the call [https://api.directx.ca/zpl/{account}/{jobID}](#operation/zpl)

 An example of the endpoint is: 

 `https://api.directx.ca/zpl/1000/12345`
 ## 5 Get reference 
 An example of calling the reference fields of a given account is: 

 `https://api.directx.ca/referenceFields/1000`
 ## 6 Track an order 
 Examples of API calls to use are: 
 * `https://api.directx.ca/order/1000/12345` 
 * `https://api.directx.ca/track/12345`

# Authentication
DirectIT API uses __API Key__ for authorization. This API key is a special token that the client needs to provide when making API calls. The key is sent as a request header under the name __sso_token__.
When authenticating to the DirectIT API, a sso token should be generated uniquely by a __server-side application__ and included as a __sso_token__ in the header of each request.
## Accessing your SSO Token
You can retrieve a __SSO Token__ by entering your credentials and account info in the [generateLoginToken](#operation/generateLoginToken) call. Your sso token will then be emailed to you.
## Using SSO Token
SSO Token are placed in the __header__
Property `sso_token` defines the token being used. 

In Curl
```
curl -X GET "https://api.directx.ca/orders/1000" -H  "accept: application/json" -H  "sso_token: xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx"
```

In Java:
```
xhr.setRequestHeader("sso_token","xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx");
```
In Python:
```
headers = {
  'sso_token': 'xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx'
}
```

post.description: Get quote for delivery. Check to see if address is valid and returns the rate choices for the delivery.

pieces.description: Number of pieces in shipment. Can affect rate and available service types

weight.description: Total weight of the shipment. Can affect rate and available service types

declared_value.description: Insurable amount.  will affect final rate if non-zero.

pickup_datetime.description: The date and time in UTC values when the trip will be ready for pickup.  Leaving null will default to ready immediately.

reference.description: Customer reference for trip. Appears on invoice.

special_instructions.description: Any specific instructions for this trip.  Will be visible to dispatch and driver.

delivery_url.description: Any web link that needs to be present on the trip, similar to a customer reference. Visible to driver.

delivery_sig_required.description: Defaults to 1.  setting to 0 will allow delivery without a signature.  (signature is always attempted)

description.description: description of the trip.  suitable examples would be 'Envelope, Box, Roll, Skid, etc'

account_email.description: will send shipment notifications to this email address including exception and delivery notifications

client_email.description: will send a notification to this email of a shipment bound to them as the receiver with a tracking link.

description.description: The company or individual name of the receiver.

suite.description: The suite portion of the address.

address.description: The road portion of the address.

city.description: The city for delivery. If left blank, will default to account default setting as specified when api key setup.

province.description: The province.  if left blank, will default to account default setting as specified when api key setup.

country.description: The country.  if left blank, will default to account default setting as specified when api key setup.

postal.description: the postal code for the address. Not required.

latitude.description: Latitude. If known, you may provide the GPS coordinates of the delivery address using these fields.  not required.

longitude.description: Longitude.  if known, you may provide the GPS coordinates of the delivery address using these fields.  not required.

contactName.description: An Attention to name at the delivery.

contactPhone.description: A phone number to contact @ the delivery.

quoteID.description: Quote ID of the order request. Valid for 60 seconds to call execute_quote

descr.example: Air Service Overnight VPU  Today after 12:57 [ $34.38]
descr.description: Description of rate and delivery method

post.description: Execute quote and place order using the quote ID retrieved from getQuote

quoteID.description: A quoteID obtained from the output of a call to /getQuote.

trackingNumber.description: Tracking Number for users to track their package

x-codeSamples.source:
curl --request POST \
  --url https://api.directx.ca/order \
  --header 'accept: text/plain' \
  --header 'content-type: application/*+json' \
  --data '{"account": "1000","shipAddressID": 1111,"conAddressID": 1111,"del_inst": "Leave at door","cust_ref": "John Doe","checkedEmail": true,"deliveryEmail": "johndoe@example.com","rate": {"baseRate": 3,"ser_time": 10,"ser_area": "S","dtdct": "2019-08-24T14:15:22Z","dtCallIn": "2019-08-24T14:15:22Z"},"email": "user@example.com","pieces": 0,"weight": 0,"packageType": "Envelope","caller": "string","additionalEmail": "user@example.com","returnTripServic": {"baseRate": 0,"ser_time": 0,"dtdct": "2019-08-24T14:15:22Z"}}'

import requests

url = "https://api.directx.ca/order"

payload = "{\"account\": \"1000\",\"shipAddressID\": 1111,\"conAddressID\": 1111,\"del_inst\": \"Leave at door\",\"cust_ref\": \"John Doe\",\"checkedEmail\": true,\"deliveryEmail\": \"johndoe@example.com\",\"rate\": {\"baseRate\": 3,\"ser_time\": 10,\"ser_area\": \"S\",\"dtdct\": \"2019-08-24T14:15:22Z\",\"dtCallIn\": \"2019-08-24T14:15:22Z\"},\"email\": \"user@example.com\",\"pieces\": 0,\"weight\": 0,\"packageType\": \"Envelope\",\"caller\": \"string\",\"additionalEmail\": \"user@example.com\",\"returnTripServic\": {\"baseRate\": 0,\"ser_time\": 0,\"dtdct\": \"2019-08-24T14:15:22Z\"}}"
headers = {
    'accept': "text/plain",
    'content-type': "application/*+json

  --url https://api.directx.ca/orderUpdateWeight/1000/12345 \
  --header 'accept: text/plain' \
  --header 'content-type: application/json' \
 --data '"newWeight":12'

import requests

url = "https://api.directx.ca/orderUpdateWeight/1000/12345"

payload = "[{\"newWeight\":12]"
headers = {
    'accept': "text/plain",
    'content-type': "application/json"
    }

response = requests.request("PATCH", url, data=payload, headers=headers)

print(response.text)

jobID.description: Job ID used to identify the order

TrackNumber.description: TrackNumber used to access information on the ship.directx

TrackingLink.description: Trackinglink to visit site for more information on status of order

sQuad.description: Quad if there exists one (NE, SE, NW, SW)

cQuad.description: Quad if there exists one (NE, SE, NW, SW)

Exception.description: Indicates if trip has an exception flag enabled. Output is 0 if false. Any other digit >= 1 is true.

Ready At Time.description: Time the package should be ready for pickup

Delivery Completion Time.description: Estimated time of completion of delivery

Declared Value.description: The insurable value of the package

zInvoice.description: Any additional charges applied to the trip (distance, fuel, etc)

quad.description: Quad if there exists one (NE, SE, NW, SW)

gpserror.description: Returns true if there is GPS error

private.description: Returns true if address is private
